message:
db: probe paths treat WAL-recovery errors as retryable-unhealthy, not hard error

Second-pass audit of the GH#99 verdict-flip fix found a remaining gap.
is_corruption_error_message no longer includes "wal file too small" (so
the verdict engine can't classify the header-only WAL state as corrupt),
but sqlite_primary_read_path_is_healthy was only catching corruption +
snapshot-conflict errors at its four probe sites — every other error
propagated up as Err, which probe_db_file_sanity still maps to Broken.
That meant a runtime WAL shrinking back to 32 bytes could still trip
the Broken verdict, just via a different code path than before.

- Open site (line ~4099): if the first open fails with a recovery-error
  (not a corruption-error), run cleanup_empty_wal_sidecar once and
  retry the open. Header-only WAL sidecars are safe to delete (no
  frame data exists — min valid frame is 24-byte header + page_size
  bytes, and min page size is 512), so the retry either succeeds
  (healthy probe, no Broken) or fails again (Ok(false), still Broken
  but correctly so).
- Quick-check / incremental-check / ack-pending sites: widen the
  classification to include is_sqlite_recovery_error_message, so a
  WAL-recovery error on an open connection is treated as Ok(false)
  (unhealthy-but-recoverable) rather than propagating as Err. The
  integrity guard still picks these up for real recovery via its own
  is_sqlite_recovery_error_message check.

No behavior change for corruption, snapshot conflict, or OOM paths.
All 20 existing pool tests still pass, including the regression
pinning "wal file too small" as recovery-but-not-corruption.
